skills.name: @jwt-auth
guidelines: Issue access tokens (short TTL) and refresh tokens (long TTL) separately
Verify JWT on every protected route with a dependency injector
Return 401 on invalid/expired tokens, 403 on insufficient scope
Use RS256 or HS256 with a secret read from env, never hardcoded
Add /refresh and /logout endpoints

skills.name: @secure-password
guidelines: Hash passwords with bcrypt (cost factor 12+) or passlib CryptContext
Never store or log plaintext passwords
Add time-bound reset tokens for forgot-password flow
Enforce minimum 8 chars, reject obvious passwords

skills.name: @rate-limiting
guidelines: Apply per-IP rate limits to /login and /register (e.g. 10 req/min)
Return HTTP 429 with Retry-After header on abuse
Log rate-limit events for monitoring
Consider slowloris and burst mitigation

skills.name: @input-validation
guidelines: Validate all user inputs with Pydantic models or Zod
Reject unexpected fields (use strict/forbid extra in Pydantic)
Sanitize string inputs to prevent XSS and SQLi
Return 422 with clear field-level error messages

skills.name: @fastapi-best-practices
guidelines: Structure: backend/app/main.py, backend/app/routes/*.py, backend/app/models/*.py
Use Pydantic BaseModel for request and response schemas
Enable CORS with explicit origins — avoid wildcard in production
Use lifespan context manager for startup/shutdown events
Mount routers with include_router and a versioned prefix like /api/v1

skills.name: @django-best-practices
guidelines: Use Django REST Framework with serializers and ViewSets
Configure INSTALLED_APPS and REST_FRAMEWORK settings in settings.py
Use Django's auth system or JWT via djangorestframework-simplejwt
Store secrets in environment variables via python-decouple or django-environ

skills.name: @express-best-practices
guidelines: Structure: src/app.js, src/routes/*.js, src/middleware/*.js, src/models/*.js
Use express-validator for input validation
Apply helmet.js for security headers and cors() for CORS
Handle async errors with express-async-errors or a global error handler

skills.name: @postgres-best-practices
guidelines: Use SQLAlchemy (Python) or Knex/Prisma (Node) with connection pooling
Store DATABASE_URL in environment, never hardcode credentials
Add indexes on frequently queried columns (user_id, email, created_at)
Use database migrations — Alembic for Python, Knex/Prisma migrate for Node

skills.name: @mongodb-best-practices
guidelines: Use Mongoose (Node) or Motor (Python async) with schema validation
Define indexes for query performance
Store MONGODB_URI in environment
Use transactions for multi-document writes when atomicity matters

skills.name: @migrations
guidelines: Create a migrations/ directory with versioned scripts
Initial migration should create users table and core schemas
Add README instructions for running migrations

skills.name: @react-forms
guidelines: Use controlled inputs with useState hooks
Show inline validation errors per field
Disable submit button during async calls to prevent double-submit
Use React Context or a state management library for auth state

skills.name: @react-api-client
guidelines: Centralize API calls in a services/ or api/ directory
Use Axios interceptors or a custom fetch wrapper for auth headers
Handle loading, error, and success states for every request
Refresh JWT token automatically on 401 before retrying

skills.name: @tailwind-setup
guidelines: Import Tailwind via CDN for quick start or configure tailwind.config.js
Use responsive utility classes (sm: md: lg:) for all layout
Ensure dark/light mode compatibility with dark: variants
Use consistent spacing scale (p-4, m-2 etc.) throughout

skills.name: @websocket-realtime
guidelines: Use FastAPI WebSocket or Socket.io for real-time events
Authenticate WS connections on upgrade (token in query param or first message)
Handle disconnect/reconnect gracefully with exponential backoff
Broadcast messages via a shared connection manager

skills.name: @error-handling
guidelines: Return consistent error shape: { error: string, detail?: any }
Never leak stack traces or internal paths to clients
Log errors server-side with context (user id, route, timestamp)
Use custom exception classes for domain errors

skills.name: @environment-config
guidelines: Load all secrets from environment variables — never commit .env files
Fail fast with a clear error message if required env vars are missing
Document all required vars in .env.example
Use different defaults for dev vs production
